methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MaverickX is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Eric Herrera and Jesse Evans that has raised $19M to date. The valuation exercise draws on a comparables universe of 9 private firms and 1 public peer (LNZA) in the climate and energy sector, though a sufficiently narrow peer cohort at the same stage and valuation tier could not be constructed from available data.

Given the constraint on peer-median analysis, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised of $19M as a proxy base value and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from seed to the next institutional stage yields an implied valuation of $48M. This approach assumes MaverickX follows conventional step-up patterns observed across seed-stage venture financings in the sector.

The triangulated valuation range spans $40M to $55M, with a median point of $48M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty. The absence of tightly comparable seed-stage climate-tech peers limits conviction in the step-up multiple; sector-specific dynamics, burn rate, and technology differentiation relative to the broader 9-company climate peer set remain unquantified drivers of actual pricing. Near-term catalysts tied to technical milestones or customer adoption would be primary inputs for calibrating entry or follow-on investment decisions.
